Who? The question who is one of the most often asked questions in everyday life. When someone tells you about a new movie you ask who's in it. When someone tells you about a new book you ask who wrote it. And when someone tells you about a party or an event you ask who's going to be there. When the answer is the right name you're anxious to participate. So what makes the right name? Certain celebrities, authors and personalities are seen as influencers. People who attract other people based on their fame, their achievements, their reputation or even their personality. Others see these people as special. It may be a special name only in their neighborhood or social circle. It might be a special name in their city, state or even around the world. These are the names that people look up to, want to be around, want to engage with. That's why having the right name in response to the question who, in business today, is crucial. We're right here in my hometown of Orlando. One of the names that's often the answer to that question who is Disney. Now obviously everyone can't be Walt Disney, but everyone can create a name within a very targeted market. That's so top of mind, that's so powerful that it too will attract customers and clients from all around the world. When you don't create a name for yourself, when you don't have a good answer to who, people have absolutely no reason to come to you. Imagine seeing a bank with just the word bank on the front or legal firm with just law firm written on the door. You don't because everyone gives their business a real name. That name is powerless unless you have a face behind it. Unless you systematically build your personal brand, your business will remain just as anonymous as if it didn't have a name. Without a personal brand, people only hire you by referral or by accident. They don't know to seek you out because they have no idea who you are. You might get by without a personal brand, but there's going to be a very low ceiling on your growth and your business is going to lag without you even knowing why. Instead, imagine how much more you can expand your business, increase your profits and how much more influence you'll have in your community and your industry if people who you don't know know who you are and what you stand for. Just imagine, motivated buyers coming directly to your door wanting to buy from you without you even having to sell yourself to them. When people are taught to seek you out, the tables are turned and you're in the position of accepting or rejecting them based on your terms, not theirs. We're working with someone who's built exactly that kind of name value for himself. We'll tell you who in just a few minutes and you're going to be amazed at what we're doing with them. My partners and I founded the celebrity branding agency based on one simple principle, that people buy people. People buy from people they know, like and trust. So we built this agency on that one simple concept and now we're the largest personal branding agency in the world. We have more than 2,000 clients in over 33 countries all around the world and we wanted to help them do one simple thing. We want to help them become the celebrity expert in their marketplace, whether that marketplace is a street corner or the globe, so that they can have the most impact in their market. We've done that by helping them become best-selling authors. We've helped them go out to Hollywood and receive Quillie Awards from the National Academy of Best-selling Authors. We've helped them speak at major universities all around the country. We've helped them get on major TV like the Biography Channel, on NBC, CBS, ABC and Fox. We've helped them get major big print publications like USA Today, Wall Street Journal, Newsweek, Forbes Magazine and many more. And we've even helped them get on over a thousand major.coms all around the world to help them spread their message for that one purpose, to help them grow their business. We don't do this for ego. We do this to help you grow your business because we want to be able to help you reach out and help even more people. And that's what we do here at the Celebrity Branding Agency. Achieving this celebrity status for our clients has helped them up their game and increase their value in the marketplace because they now have become a name to be reckoned with. Now it's time to take the Celebrity Brand up a notch, to not only become the most recognized player in your industry, but to create your own industry. People like Donald Trump, Hugh Hefner, Richard Branson and even the Kardashians, they built their businesses not only on what they know, but most importantly on who they are. When you display that expertise, first of all, you climb those rungs on the ladder. You tower over everyone else in your field as an expert. And when consumers are looking for you, or if it's B2B, same thing, business people looking for you, you are all they see. And then kind of taking that to the next level is kind of the idea of a generalist and a specialist. And then going from specialist to celebrity, and then from celebrity to legend. I mean, let's talk about generalists. I mean, generalists get general business, right? Specialists get specialist business. You get paid for it, right? Dan Kennedy gets paid what he gets paid, because he is a specialist. He is a direct response copywriter. He works in certain fields, and he knows them really well. If he was just writing copy for cat food and dog food and real estate, I mean, he's a very specialist. And that's why he gets paid what he gets paid. But he has the celebrity on top of it. He has the legendary sales top. But let's kind of talk about why most business owners that are probably watching this start out kind of as generalists and why that's kind of hurting their income and their business growth. Yeah, well, all of us with whatever it is that we do, we know we can help a lot more people. We know we can help a lot of people. So most people go into business because they want to fill a need. They've got a burning desire. And they go out in the marketplace, and they start just rifling things, or shocking things all over the place. And they're like, why is no one coming to me? It's because in order to get someone to pay attention to your message, you've got to get it to them multiple times. And you may not even remember who the first person you gave your message to. And so you might stand up in a meeting and say, hey, I can help you with anything you need. Is this technically what you're saying? I'm a general practice doctor. So any problem, come to me. I'm a marketer. Anything you need with marketing, come to me. Well, you may or may not get someone who's desperate enough to like, well, I've got a need, and I can't find someone else. I'll go talk to this guy. But you just set yourself up for failure essentially because you cannot afford the time, energy, effort, dollars to reach everyone. So what you have just got to niche. And Dan's talked about it before a million times, riches and niches. And so the first thing you have to do is you have to niche down, become a specialist. A brain surgeon could probably very likely, at least has gone through the schooling, a lot of it to practice general medicine. But would you rather have the hourly billing collection of a brain surgeon or a general practice doctor? I can guess we'd all say brain surgeon. And it really is because that person has niche down specified and they have gotten. The deeper you get into a niche, the more you get paid. Because fewer and fewer people who understand it. So if I'm a direct response operator that specializes in cat food for over age and overweight cats, I don't know what it is. But if you got this food for these kind of cats, if there's a big enough market, you're coming to me, right? Exactly. And you're going to pay the number if I can prove what I do works because at the end of the day, again, another thing, Dan says, when we get into the right nature, we have the right offer. We're just selling money at a discount. People can buy thousands of dollars for less money. I'll just hire you, right? Exactly. So let's kind of put this into practice. And this really happened in your own story because you got out of law school and you kind of didn't really want to be a lawyer. But you're a lawyer anyway. So you had that bottom level, right? You had, you passed the bar, you're going through this process. And now you've got to figure out, well, how am I going to make a living for myself and for my family and for my kids? So let's kind of talk about how you went from really being a generalist to being a specialist to becoming the celebrity expert, the Emmy Award winning director, the best selling author, and all these things. How did that transformation take place? So the key to all that, first of all, yeah, I went to law school with the intent of never practicing a day of law in my life. I broke that. That sounds real smart. Yeah, I broke that. But it's what it is. But I want to be the president of a record label at the time. I knew that the presidents of a lot of major corporations were lawyers. And so I went to law school. And my brother went to med school. So I had to do something. So I went to law school. And I went through school. And I finished. I got married after my first semester of law school. My wife and I have been married 12 years now. I had my first son a week after I took the bar exam. So life was not heavy and fast, right? And so I went to my business partner, Jack, now, who's my mentor at the time, and said, Jack, what should I do? And he said, well, Nick, I know you never want to practice law, but here's the thing. From your background, so throughout college and before that, I was producing records, directing music videos. Not very good ones, by the way, those directing music videos. I was managing bands. I was doing a lot of stuff. I'd gotten the opportunity to work with Bill Cosby and President Bush and a legendary basketball coach, Bobby Knight, football legend, Don Shula. I had to work with lots of people through the university. And I got to either work on the marketing campaigns or personally work with them, whatever it was. And he said, look, you have a distinct skill set in media creation. I've been creating all types of media. And you've learned from some of the best on celebrity buzz building and PR. I said, yeah, you're right. He said, well, what you might not know about me, Nick, is that my specialty, he had marketed and sold over a billion dollars for the products and services through personal branding and direct marketing. Said, Nick, I think if we do this correctly, we combine all four of our skill sets. Personal branding, direct marketing, celebrity buzz building, and media creation, we can position you as one of the top entertainment lawyers in the country. I said, well, that's better than just being a lawyer. So we said I have to do that. And within a year of me practicing full time, I was building it over three times the rate of anyone I knew who'd been practicing law for 20, 30 years in New York or LA. And we're in Orlando. Not the place matters that much more. But the bulk of the entertainment workers are going to come to New York, LA, and Nashville. So I did what any intelligent 20-something-year-old would do, who's earning a lot of money per hour. I went to Jack and said, hey, I don't do this anymore. I said, well, that's brilliant. What do you want to do now? By the way, I had baby number two on the way now. And he said, and I figured out what caused that problem, by the way. So I fixed it. I have three, and I'm done. No more kids. And so I went to Jack and I said, what I want to do is I want to teach people how to do this, because this really is a freedom formula. A lot of the things that we are revealing in the living legend formula that you're going to be seeing in these videos as you see them, this process that Dan and I have created to help you become this living legend, but it's really a freedom formula. It'll help you save time or keep more of your time, make more money, or whatever you want to do. It helps you get that freedom. Right. It really creates autonomy. And that's why we're all in business for ourselves. We want autonomy. That's truly the secret. So we wrote a book, Celebrity Branding You. There's a long process of how that happened. But we wrote a book called Celebrity Branding You. A secret. I'll tell everyone here today, if you want more credibility right now, figure out the book you've always been talking about writing in your head, give it a title, and add to your bio that you're the author of the forthcoming book, XYZ. That's a big takeaway right now. Huge credibility right there. You'll get huge credibility just because people will say, why are you writing a book? Tell me more about it. Now you're going to have to write the book. You actually have to work on it. But it will get you motivated. It'll give you a huge bit of credibility. So we wrote the book.
